title: WEST Act of 2025
plain_summary: The WEST Act of 2025 creates a new tax on very rich colleges and universities. These schools must pay a six percent tax if they have saved more than ten or eleven billion dollars. The exact amount depends on what type of school it is. The tax starts in 2025 and is based on all the money and investments the school has saved up over time.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Woke Endowment Security Tax Act of two thousand twenty five or the WEST Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Excise Tax on Certain Large Private College and University Endowments
sections.text: This section adds a new tax rule to the tax code. Starting in two thousand twenty five, certain large private colleges and universities must pay a special tax on their endowments (the money and investments they have saved up). The tax is six percent of the total value of their assets from the previous year. This tax applies to private colleges and universities that are not religious schools and have assets worth at least eleven billion nine hundred million dollars. It also applies to colleges that work for a state government under state law or contracts and have assets worth at least ten billion five hundred million dollars. The tax does not count assets that the school uses directly for its educational mission. The rules for counting assets and students follow the same rules as an existing tax law. This new tax starts for tax years beginning after December thirty first two thousand twenty four.
